hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The output consists of plain text lines in the format: <subject> <predicate> <object> . where objects are optionally quoted literals, followed by a dot and a space.
The system uses the specific strings 'error=keyword_redefinition' when context keyword definitions conflict, and 'error=loading_remote_context_failed' when failing to fetch a remote context URL.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the dotted triple format defined in the text conversion module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The output must use the pattern 'subject predicate object .' without spaces after the dot, representing RDF triples in N-Triples format.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_expand.json.description: Expand compact JSON-LD documents into full IRI-based node objects while applying context aliases, type coercion, and default language rules.

feature2_compact.json.description: Compact expanded JSON-LD using a supplied context so IRIs, types, native values, and context declarations are rendered in their shortest valid JSON-LD form.

feature3_flatten.json.description: Flatten JSON-LD into a deterministic node list that preserves graph membership, native values, and embedded graph data after expansion.

feature4_frame.json.description: Frame JSON-LD by selecting graph nodes that match a frame and embedding or referencing related nodes according to the frame and processing options.

feature5_to_rdf.json.description: Convert JSON-LD documents into RDF statements, including literal language tags, RDF type triples, RDF lists, typed literals, and decimal lexical values.

http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#lab.@value: A plain literal with a lang tag.

cases.expected_output: _:b0 <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label> "A plain literal with a lang tag."@en-us .

feature6_from_rdf.json.description: Convert N-Quads RDF input into expanded JSON-LD while reconstructing lists, blank-node structures, named graphs, native values, and RDF type handling.

feature7_normalize.json.description: Normalize JSON-LD into canonical N-Quads so blank node identifiers and statement ordering are stable for equivalent RDF datasets.

feature8_namespaces.json.description: Preserve valid namespace prefixes during RDF conversion and choose compact names that shorten IRIs without losing prefix mappings.

feature9_errors.json.description: Report invalid JSON-LD contexts and invalid IRI values as normalized language-neutral error categories instead of runtime exception details.

_constraints.output_rule: Output must be a JSON array of parsed objects with fully expanded IRIs. Object values MUST be arrays of literals or objects.

_constraints.input_condition: algorithm is 'compact' and document is expanded IRIs
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be a JSON object. Context MUST be included in the output document using supply mode (root level).
Output must be a JSON array of single nodes. The @graph key from the input document MUST be replaced by this top-level array.
Output must be a JSON object (single root level). The frame defines embedded sections where reference sections are flattened into arrays of objects.
Output must be plain text lines describing RDF triples. Use 'subject predicate object .' format.
Output must be a JSON array representing the subject graph node. Lists of values must be wrapped in objects with '@value', '@type', or '@language'.
Output must be plain text lines representing normalized N-Quads. Empty output (only newline) allowed for empty documents.

_constraints.input_condition: algorithm is 'to_rdf' OR 'roundtrip_namespaces' AND option or flag 'namespaces' or 'useNamespaces' is true
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be key=value lines declaring prefixes mapping.

_constraints.input_condition: Validation fails based on @context structure or remote context loading
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be a single line string prefixed with 'error=' and the error category code.

_constraints.input_condition: Input document is empty or null (after stripping whitespace)
_constraints.output_rule: Must not produce output for Expand/Compact unless explicitly handling empty nodes (per JSON-LD spec), but for Normalize output defined as text lines.
